channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hungary's transformation into an 'electoral autocracy' has parallels to Trump's second term
item.description: U.S. conservatives have looked to Hungary's Viktor Orbán as an inspiration for a right-wing America, and the prime minister has become a close ally of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike And Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: Some House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges blocking Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Musk's latest moves to shrink the federal workforce explained
item.description: President Trump and right-hand man Elon Musk have rolled out a series of moves to cull the federal workforce, forging ahead with numerous methods to fire employees that have already sparked legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Here's what happened during President Donald Trump's 4th week in office
item.description: President Donald Trump and his administration are working to negotiate an end to the war between Russia and Ukraine, and everything else that happened his fourth week in his second administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fearful immigrants ask Florida activist to sign guardianship papers for their children
item.description: HOMESTEAD, Fla. (AP) — The day before President Donald Trump’s inauguration, a dozen immigrant families came to Nora Sandigo's ranch to ask her to be a legal guardian of their children. Now they are insisting she come over to their homes to sign the necessary paperwork.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alleged DOGE employees show up at San Francisco City Hall
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- An official with the San Francisco Sheriff’s Office confirmed to NewsNation affiliate KRON4 that it is investigating a report of three individuals claiming to be Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) employees who attempted to enter offices in San Francisco City Hall on
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE says it dug up another $1.9 billion in taxpayer money 'misplaced' by Biden admin
item.description: The Department of Government Efficient on Friday said it continues to find wasteful spending across the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio greenlights $7M+ in waivers to keep border security efforts in Mexico alive amid funding freeze
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is using waivers to keep millions in funding going to Mexico for vital programs that target the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. and target cartels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: Federal workers were responding with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration’s latest effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protections.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Brian Witte, Mike Householder And Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump rolls back CFPB, USAID and Department of Education: Where things stand
item.description: President Trump supercharged his overhaul of government agencies this week that included a fresh round of a widespread firings.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams’ corruption charges won’t be dropped soon: Attorney
item.description: Seven officials have resigned since the order from the Justice Department to drop the charges against Mayor Adams.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump official considering running for California governor if Kamala Harris enters race
item.description: Richard Grenell, a Trump administration official, said Friday that he would consider entering the 2026 California governor race if former Vice President and Democratic Presidential candidate Kamala Harris joins the growing list of candidates.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith announces she won't seek reelection in 2026
item.description: Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith (D) will not run for reelection in 2026, opening up a seat in the Democratic-leaning state, she announced Thursday. Smith said in a video posted to the social platform X that she has “loved” her job as a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: The art of the deal? Zelenskyy says a Ukraine-Russia agreement must come through Trump negotiations
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy appealed to President Donald Trump's history as a dealmaker on Friday, offering flattering words as he sought the U.S. president's leadership in negotiations that could end the Russia-Ukraine conflict.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, MONIKA SCISLOWSKA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell tests the strengths and limits of his power opposing a trio of Trump's Cabinet nominees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No longer in charge, Sen. Mitch McConnell has been speaking his mind, the long-serving GOP leader rejecting President Donald Trump’s more high-profile Cabinet nominees — alone at times, among the Republicans, casting his no votes.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sets a 5-day deadline for the Trump administration to start lifting its USAID funding freeze
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge has ordered President Donald Trump's administration to temporarily lift a funding freeze that has shut down U.S. humanitarian aid and development work around the world, and he has set a five-day deadline for the administration to prove it's complying.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At McMahon’s confirmation hearing, it was the Education Department on trial
item.description: In her confirmation hearing, Linda McMahon handled tough questions from Democrats, and affirmed President Trump’s plans to shrink, and potentially eliminate, the department.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump’s gender-affirming care executive orders nationwide
item.description: BALTIMORE — A federal judge Thursday blocked parts of two executive orders issued by President Trump that collectively seek to restrict gender-affirming care. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Ted Cruz’s list of ’woke’ science includes self-driving cars and solar eclipses
item.description: Sen. Ted Cruz, R-Texas, says the National Science Foundation has given money to thousands of “woke DEI” studies. Researchers say that’s misrepresenting science and disparaging important research.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. confirmed as Trump’s health secretary, over Democrats’ loud objections
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. wins confirmation to be President Trump’s secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services. The vote was 52 to 48.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota lawmaker wants to ban new billboard signs
item.description: Sen. John Marty, DFL-Roseville, said the signs detract from the state's natural scenic beauty and pose a safety concern for motorists. Billboard companies say the bill would hurt local businesses and curtail First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5 to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation
